THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Marathon County learned it was denied FEMA public assistance after the April 2026 storms, though individual aid remains available to residents. Locally, Kronenwetter and Weston boards moved forward on infrastructure spending and development decisions.

  • Marathon County was approved for FEMA individual assistance but denied public assistance due to insufficient documented damages; 24 county residents have already applied at disasterassistance.gov.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board approved an $814,815 amended bid for a six-bay equipment garage, adding a second restroom, and approved ballot drop box placement for the August primary and November general elections.
  • Weston's Public Works Committee awarded the Concord Avenue/Bayberry Street reconstruction to HS Suns for approximately $2,248,819.90, including a sidewalk alternate, and awarded a $12,500 surveying contract for Howland Avenue to Point of Beginning.
  • Weston's Plan Commission unanimously approved a conditional use permit for a 1,090 sq ft detached accessory dwelling unit at 4804 Shore Avenue, with short-term rental prohibited, and forwarded approval of the 23-lot Bull Junior View subdivision plat to the village board.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board directed staff to prepare a moratorium resolution on data centers and solar farms for the next board agenda, and referred election ordinance revisions to the CLIP committee.
  • Kronenwetter's Plan Commission is set to consider rezoning Marathon County Highway Department property from B3, SF and AR to M2 General Industrial, and a conditional use permit for the Knetter pond, at its revised Monday meeting.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate, including Weston's Board of Trustees at 6:00 p.m. and Kronenwetter's revised Plan Commission at 6:00 p.m., where zoning and development requests are on the agenda. Wausau's Plan Commission meets Tuesday at 5:00 p.m., and the city's Historic Preservation Commission convenes Wednesday at 4:30 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
